topic: 1Password Unified Access for AI Agents: Developer Security Guide

key_points: Unified Access Pro launched March 17, 2026 — discover, secure, and audit across human, machine, and AI agent identities
Three pillars: Discover (surface exposed SSH keys, .env files, AI agent activity), Secure (centralize credentials in vault), Audit (unified access trail)
Runtime credential delivery: inject secrets into agents at task time, not persistently stored on disk
Partnerships with Anthropic (Claude), Cursor, GitHub, Perplexity, Vercel at launch
SDKs available for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Go
Tutorial: AI agent books a flight and submits expense report using Anthropic Claude — zero hardcoded secrets
Use secrets.resolve() with secret reference URIs to inject credentials at runtime
Create a dedicated vault + service account with read-only access scoped only to agent-needed items
MCP server integration allows Claude and other AI assistants to pull credentials from 1Password vault via Model Context Protocol
Prevents credential exposure in agent configuration files
op run command injects secrets at runtime without embedding tokens in config
BeyondTrust frames AI agents as 'new insider threat' if overprivileged
Recommends treating agents as first-class identities with scoped authentication
Emphasizes just-in-time access and ephemeral credentials over static API keys
HashiCorp Vault: comprehensive but high operational overhead, steep learning curve
Doppler: developer-first, strong DX but narrower identity scope
Akeyless: cloud-native SaaS, Distributed Fragments Cryptography, zero-knowledge architecture
1Password differentiator: only platform combining consumer UX, developer secrets, AND agentic AI identity in one product

stats.fact: 1Password is trusted by over 180,000 businesses, 1 million developers, and secures 1.3 billion human and machine credentials
1Password surpassed $400M ARR in October 2025, with 75%+ of revenue from businesses and 90%+ gross retention
30%+ of Fortune 100 and over two-thirds of the Forbes AI 50 use 1Password
Gartner: by 2026, 30% of enterprises will rely on AI agents that act independently, triggering transactions and completing tasks autonomously
Non-human identities (service principals, secrets, agents) outnumber human users 100-to-1 in enterprise environments
GitGuardian 2025 report: 24 million leaked credentials found on GitHub; 70% of secrets leaked in 2022 are still valid today
1Password acquired a secrets-management company for $75 million in August 2025 to strengthen its developer-focused API key governance
1Password Unified Access Pro became generally available on March 17, 2026, with launch partners Anthropic, Cursor, GitHub, Perplexity, and Vercel

angles: Developer quickstart: how to securely inject secrets into AI agents using 1Password SDKs (Python/JS/Go) — no hardcoded credentials
Unified identity layer: why treating AI agents as first-class identities (not just service accounts) is the new security standard
MCP integration deep-dive: using 1Password as the secrets backend for Claude, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools
Real cost of credential sprawl: connect GitGuardian stats on leaked secrets to why runtime injection matters for autonomous agents
1Password vs alternatives: when to use Unified Access Pro vs HashiCorp Vault vs Doppler vs Akeyless for AI workloads
Zero-trust for agentic AI: how Discover → Secure → Audit covers the full agent identity lifecycle
Human-in-the-loop approval flows: 1Password's consent model for agents accessing sensitive credentials
